behavior_in_task_description.reason: The instruction names all critical paths (hub-a/b/c page.tsx, dynamic-stale-10/60, test file path, LinkAccordion component path, AGENTS.md, .agents/skills/router-act/SKILL.md), required content patterns (LinkAccordion, connection(), Suspense, export default function Page(), hub links, browser.back() absence), and structural requirements that map to test assertions. Specific details like 'data-link-accordion' attribute are part of the pre-existing component checked by pass-to-pass tests, not fail

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction essentially specifies the complete fix: create three hub pages at exact paths with exact structure, modify stale pages to add LinkAccordion links to hubs, replace browser.back() with hub navigation in the test, create AGENTS.md and SKILL.md with specific content. This is not describing what's broken — it's a detailed implementation specification that leaves almost no reasoning for the agent.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert very specific implementation details: test_hub_pages_structure checks for exact string 'export default function Page()' (not 'export default function' with any name), exact content like 'await connection()', exact paths hub-a/hub-b/hub-c. test_router_act_skill_created checks for specific strings like 'name: router-act', 'no-requests', 'hub', 'includes', 'requestIdleCallback', 'BFCache'. An alternative correct fix using different function names, different skill naming, or different d

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The fail-to-pass tests (test_hub_pages_structure, test_stale_pages_link_to_hubs, test_test_uses_hub_navigation, test_agents_md_router_act_rule, test_router_act_skill_created) are almost entirely string-in-file checks: reading file content and asserting substrings exist. The Node.js script in test_hub_pages_structure does fs.readFileSync and checks content.includes() — pure text matching, not code execution or behavior verification.

test_not_tautological.reason: The fail-to-pass tests can be passed by creating files with the right strings without any actual bug fix. For example, test_hub_pages_structure just checks that files contain 'LinkAccordion', 'await connection()', '<Suspense', 'export default function Page()' as substrings. A file with those strings in comments would pass. The actual flaky test behavior is never executed.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All behaviors listed in instruction.md (hub pages structure, stale page connectivity, test navigation assertions, AGENTS.md documentation, SKILL.md creation) have corresponding test functions.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with clear section comments (Pass-to-pass static, Pass-to-pass repo_tests, Fail-to-pass pr_diff code behavior, Fail-to-pass config/instruction), descriptive function names, and docstrings explaining each check.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task does not produce structured data like JSON/CSV/API/DB output.

no_typos.reason: File paths, commands, and variable names in the instruction and tests are consistent and correctly spelled.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction specifies exact file paths to create/modify, exact content patterns required (e.g., 'export default function Page()', 'await connection()'), exact navigation patterns to use in the test ('Navigate to /per-page-config/hub-a, hub-b, hub-c instead of browser.back()'), and exact documentation content. This goes well beyond describing the bug domain.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: The AGENTS.md and SKILL.md are new files being created (not full-file rewrites of existing config), and the test file modification is a localized change replacing browser.back() with hub navigation in one specific test.
